title: 10 best herbal essential oils for blocked nose
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for blocked nose are natural remedies derived from plants that help ease nasal congestion by opening airways and reducing inflammation. They work by releasing decongestant properties when inhaled, providing quick relief from blocked noses. The best herbal essential oils for blocked nose include Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Thymus vulgaris, Rosmarinus officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Lavandula angustifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Hyptis suaveolens, Zingiber officinale, and Ocimum basilicum. If left untreated, blocked nose can cause fatigue, difficulty sleeping, and decreased productivity, significantly impacting one's quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains eucalyptol, a compound that breaks down mucus and reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing instant relief from congestion. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. This natural remedy provides fast and effective relief from blocked noses, making it a popular choice for colds, flu, and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Decongestant property: The oil's main compound, cineole, helps to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure by constricting blood vessels and breaking down mucus.
Expectorant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the feeling of blockage.
Anti-inflammatory property: The oil's anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to blocked noses and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum contain expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving congestion and clearing mucus from the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, providing relief from blocked noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted supplier or your own garden weighing approximately one kilogram.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for four to six hours or until they reach 10% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using a double boiler and condenser at a temperature of 100°c for two to three hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue using a separating funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities or sediment before use within six months of production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's compounds, such as thymol and borneol, have been shown to help reduce inflammation and open up airways, allowing for easier breathing. When inhaled, these compounds can help dissolve mucus and relieve congestion, providing fast and effective relief from blocked noses. Additionally, the warm and soothing aroma of thyme essential oil can also help to calm the mind and promote relaxation, making it a natural and comforting remedy for sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_properties: Decongestant properties: Thyme essential oil helps to reduce nasal congestion by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the nasal tissues, which can help alleviate symptoms of a blocked nose such as redness, swelling, and itching.
Antimicrobial properties: Thyme essential oil has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to combat infections that can cause a blocked nose, such as sinusitis or colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are often used in aromatherapy blends to help relieve congestion and sinus pressure due to their pungent, earthy flavor profile.
Leaves: Fresh or dried thyme leaves are commonly used to make tea or tinctures that help ease respiratory issues, including nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn or early morning when their oils are most concentrated.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them with distilled water and blotting dry with a paper towel.
Steam-distill the leaves using a copper still and 300ml of pure water for 1 hour to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to separate the oil from water and debris.
Store the filtered thymus vulgaris essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains cineole, a compound that effectively reduces nasal congestion by loosening mucus and promoting drainage. When inhaled through steam inhalation or applied topically to the chest, tea tree oil's decongestant effects can help ease sinus pressure and relieve stuffiness. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for alleviating cold and allergy symptoms associated with a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause sinusitis and blockages, helping to clear out the nasal passages and restore normal breathing.
Decongestant properties: Tea tree oil's decongestant properties help to reduce excess mucus production and thin out existing mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cineole, a natural decongestant that helps relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Seeds: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with a blocked nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia plants from your garden or local supplier at dawn.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set to 40°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 200g of dried leaves with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, ensuring proper coverage.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, allowing the essential oils to diffuse.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain the clear melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. When inhaled, rosemary oil stimulates the mucous membranes, helping to clear out excess mucus and reduce nasal congestion. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, further relieving pressure and discomfort associated with a blocked nose. This natural remedy can provide fast and effective relief from stuffy sinuses and promote healthy respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Helps to ease congestion and reduce nasal pressure by opening up airways and breaking down mucus, allowing for easier breathing.
Camphor: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving sinus pressure.
Cineole: Acts as a natural decongestant and expectorant, helping to thin out mucus and make it easier to expel, which can provide relief from blocked nose and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain sesquiterpenes, which help to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure, making them a great addition to essential oils for blocked nose relief.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and clearing of blockages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn when they are most aromatic.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly using a soft cloth or paper towels.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a stainless steel apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from water using a separator funnel after 30 minutes of cooling.
Transfer the obtained essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool place for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with blocked nose because its decongestant properties help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. The oil's natural antiseptic qualities also help to combat bacterial and viral infections, further alleviating sinus pressure and stuffiness. Additionally, the calming scent of English lavender has a soothing effect on the mind and body, allowing for deeper breathing and easier drainage of mucus, providing fast relief from a blocked nose.

Antihistamine-like effect: The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of English Lavender essential oil can help to relieve symptoms of allergic rhinitis, such as sneezing, runny nose, and congestion, by reducing histamine release and soothing irritated mucous membranes.

Expectorant property: English Lavender essential oil has a sweet, calming aroma that can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and promoting natural drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal essential oils for blocked nose because they contain a high concentration of camphor, which helps to thin mucus and ease congestion.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can help alleviate symptoms of a blocked nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from a sustainable source ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one cup of dried lavender flowers with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:2 for optimal extraction.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks shaking the container daily to facilitate efficient oil release and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and storing the resulting essential oil in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. When inhaled, lemongrass oil increases blood circulation to the nasal passages, reducing swelling and congestion caused by colds, allergies, or sinus infections. Its citrusy scent also helps to loosen and clear out mucus, promoting healthy drainage and relieving sinus pressure. Additionally, lemongrass's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated nasal tissues, making it an effective remedy for alleviating blocked noses and restoring normal breathing.
remedy_properties: Decongestant property: Lemongrass essential oil helps to reduce nasal congestion by thinning out mucus and promoting its easy expulsion from the body, allowing for easier breathing.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to a blocked nose.
Expectorant property: Lemongrass essential oil contains expectorants that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass are rich in citral and geraniol, which help to clear nasal passages and alleviate congestion.
Leaves: Lemongrass leaves contain a high amount of limonene, an essential oil that helps to break down mucus and reduce inflammation, making it effective for relieving blocked noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Steam distill the leaves using a still with 100ml of distilled water for 3 hours at 100°c temperature.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residue by filtering it through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Store the filtered essential oil in an airtight container for 2 weeks to allow sedimentation and separation.
Transfer the clear essential oil into a dark glass bottle and seal it tightly with a cap, ready for use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. When inhaled, peppermint oil's menthol content stimulates blood flow to the nasal passages, helping to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, reducing swelling and congestion. Expectorant property: Peppermint essential oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, making it an effective remedy for respiratory issues such as a blocked nose. Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to a blocked nose. By reducing inflammation, peppermint oil can help alleviate sinus pressure and promote easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds, peppermint roots help to decongest and clear nasal passages.
Buds: The small, immature leaves on peppermint buds contain high concentrations of menthone, which is effective in relieving sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried peppermint leaves using a still or a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate by decanting it into a clean glass container.
Store the extracted mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an airtight lid at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scots pine essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's main component, pinene, is a potent expectorant that helps to break down and eliminate mucus buildup in the nasal passages. When inhaled, Scots pine essential oil can help to thin out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion caused by colds, sinusitis, and allergies. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, providing quick and effective relief from a blocked nose.

Bark: The bark contains natural decongestants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to relieve blocked noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pinus sylvestris needles from a healthy tree, cutting stems just above the node to ensure proper regeneration.
Dry the harvested pinus sylvestris needles in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days, monitoring moisture levels carefully.
Crush the dried pinus sylvestris needles into small pieces using mortar and pestle, then transfer them to a glass jar with a lid.
Steam distill the crushed pinus sylvestris needle mixture in a glass apparatus with a condenser, collecting essential oil at a rate of 10-15 ml per hour for 3 hours.

Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter paper into a clean glass container, discarding any impurities and storing the final product in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant and expectorant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in late summer for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 48 hours.
Combine 100g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and steep it for 14 days shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container discarding solids then store in amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains calendsol, a compound that reduces inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in sufficient quantity for your desired oil production.
Dry the flowers thoroughly using low heat or air drying methods within 24 hours of harvest.
Mix one part dried calendula flowers with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass container.
Leave the mixture to macerate for 14-21 days in a cool dark place allowing for maximum extraction of essential oils.
Strain and filter the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and preserving oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat blocked nose with herbal essential oils is by inhaling the steam from a bowl of hot water mixed with a few drops of eucalyptus, peppermint, or rosemary oil. These oils have natural decongestant properties that help ease congestion and open up airways. Simply add 5-7 drops of your chosen oil to the boiling water, cover your head with a towel, and inhale the steam for 5-10 minutes, three times a day. This natural remedy provides quick relief from blocked noses and sinus pressure.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to blocked nose that are treated with herbal essential oils are sinusitis, congestion, and respiratory issues. Essential oils like eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme help to clear nasal passages and relieve sinus pressure, reducing the symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, oils such as rosemary and ginger can help to ease coughs and congestion, promoting easier breathing and overall respiratory wellness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, work to break down mucus and promote drainage, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing quick relief from stuffiness and congestion, allowing for a clear and unobstructed nasal passage.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent helps to thin mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.
Pinene: Pinene helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Bornyl acetate: This constituent helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in eucalyptol, a key compound that acts as a natural decongestant and expectorant to help clear nasal passages.
Barks: The bark contains a high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe.
Buds: The buds of Eucalyptus globulus are also a significant source of eucalyptol and other bioactive compounds that help to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves by cutting them from the plant during the summer months.
Steam distill the harvested leaves with 0.2 part of leaves to 1 part of water at 70-80°c for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation or centrifugation within 2 hours of distillation.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and improve clarity.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. When inhaled, it can help to thin mucus and promote drainage, providing relief from a blocked nose. Its antimicrobial properties also help to combat infections that can contribute to nasal congestion. This makes it a popular natural remedy for sinus issues.

Limonene: It possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
Pinene: It has decongestant and bronchodilator properties, helping to thin mucus and increase airflow, thereby providing relief from nasal congestion and blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for a blocked nose due to their high content of cineole, a compound with decongestant properties.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils for a blocked nose because they contain a high concentration of cineole, which helps to relieve nasal congestion.
Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils for a blocked nose due to their high cineole content, which provides decongestant properties to help relieve nasal congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with blocked nose because it contains thymol, a natural decongestant that effectively breaks down mucus and phlegm. remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps to thin mucus and promote its expulsion, thereby relieving congestion and blocked nose.
P-Cymene: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thus reducing nasal blockage.
Bornyl acetate: Demonstrates decongestant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for a blocked nose due to their high concentration of thymol, a natural decongestant.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are also widely used to create essential oils for a blocked nose because they contain a blend of compounds that help ease congestion and promote breathing.

Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are sometimes used to make essential oils for a blocked nose, as they contain a mixture of oils that help to relieve nasal congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh thymus vulgaris flowers and leaves in the early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and filter it using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the final thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to break down and clear mucus and debris from the nasal passages, promoting drainage and congestion relief. Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to blocked nose.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a decongestant, thinning mucus and reducing congestion, making it easier to breathe through the nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils for a blocked nose because they contain high levels of camphor and borneol, which help to ease congestion.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used to make essential oils for a blocked nose because they contain eucalyptol and pinene, which have decongestant and expectorant properties.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are also used to make essential oils for a blocked nose because they contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning to capture their highest essential oil content.
Dry the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at 35-40 degrees celsius.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers for 3-4 hours at 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil from the distillation process and filter it through a paper filter.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at 15-20 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. remedy_constituents: Menthol: A naturally occurring compound that helps to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe through a blocked nose.
Limonene: An organic compound that has natural decongestant properties, helping to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Menthone: A monoterpene with natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving blocked nose symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and stems from mature mentha x piperita plants with scissors during the early morning hours.
Steam distill 200g of the harvested material in a glass apparatus with 1.5kg of water for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a condenser and a separating funnel.
Store the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.
Analyze the essential oil using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry to ensure its quality and composition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Beta-caryophyllene: Aids in relieving blocked nose by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and open up airways.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom to ensure optimal oil content and fragrance.
Steam distill the fresh flowers at 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled steam and separate the essential oil from the water using a condenser.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its clarity and stability.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

Citral: Possesses natural decongestant properties, helping to thin mucus and promote its drainage, providing relief from a blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of essential oils in Cymbopogon citratus, containing high concentrations of citral and geraniol, which help to clear congestion and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, also contain essential oils that help to open airways and relieve nasal congestion due to their high citral content.

Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus contain essential oils that aid in decongesting the nasal passages and relieving sinus pressure, although their oil content is generally lower than that of the leaves and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 pounds of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from mature plants in early morning.
Clean the leaves by gently rinsing them with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill the leaves in a 1:2 ratio with distilled water using a steam distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil using a separatory funnel and a filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The oil contains compounds like borneol and camphor that help to break down mucus and reduce congestion. It also has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the nasal passages, reducing swelling and promoting ease of breathing. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the blocked nose, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Sabinene: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and other debris that may be clogging the nasal passages.
Limonene: Exhibits bronchodilatory properties, which can help to widen the airways and improve breathing by reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Caryophyllene: Possesses anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with a blocked nose, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Hyptis suaveolens for essential oils to relieve blocked noses, as they contain the highest concentration of essential oils with decongestant properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their fragrance and decongestant properties, which help to alleviate blocked noses and sinus congestion.
Stems: Stems are also used to make essential oils, which are rich in eugenol and other compounds that help to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom for 3 days.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release oils.
Collect the steam distillate and separate the oil from water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a charcoal filter to remove impurities and improve quality.
Store the hyptis suaveolens essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil, also known as ginger essential oil, has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages. Its warm and spicy aroma also helps to thin mucus, promoting easy drainage and alleviating stuffiness. Additionally, Zingiber officinale essential oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving blocked nose symptoms. This natural remedy offers a soothing and effective solution for nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Gingerol: It exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion.
Beta-sesquiphellandrene: It has decongestant properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion by thinning mucus and promoting drainage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural decongestant properties.
Roots: The roots are also widely used as they contain a combination of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce nasal congestion.
Buds: The buds are another part used for their high content of essential oils, particularly gingerols, which are responsible for their decongestant and expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh rhizomes, cut into small pieces to ensure optimal oil extraction.
Steam distill the rhizome pieces at 100-150°c for 2-4 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum essential oils helps with blocked nose because it contains eugenol and linalool, compounds that have natural decongestant properties. These oils help to open up airways and reduce congestion by thinning mucus and promoting drainage. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of Ocimum basilicum essential oils also help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. This natural remedy can provide relief from stuffiness and congestion, promoting a clear and easy breathing.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Acts as a natural decongestant by reducing inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, helping to relieve congestion.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the body, relieving blocked nose symptoms.
Borneol: Possesses decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion and promote the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ocimum basilicum for essential oil production due to their high concentration of volatile compounds that help to decongest the nasal passages.
Stems: Stems are also used to produce essential oils that help to clear congestion by releasing oils that aid in the dilation of airways and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Buds: Buds are used to make essential oils that contain antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the inflammation and congestion caused by a blocked nose, promoting a clear airway.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ocimum basilicum leaves when they are in full bloom and the essential oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested ocimum basilicum leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried ocimum basilicum leaves to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separating funnel.
Store the extracted ocimum basilicum essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid.
